demographics.living_situation: lives at home with family; adult child present and providing care

opening_complaint.verbatim_patient_text: My body feels like it weighs a thousand pounds, every joint in my hands is screaming, and I can barely keep my eyes open. (Adult child on the call: he's gone downhill so fast in the last couple of days, he can't even get out of bed.)
opening_complaint.stated_reason_for_presenting_today: He's been getting more and more exhausted for months, but in the last two days he's become so drowsy and confused he can't stand up or look after himself, and we're frightened.

symptom_narrative.onset: creeping exhaustion for about 12 months, dramatically worse over the last 48 hours
symptom_narrative.character: whole-body heaviness and exhaustion, aching painful knuckles in both hands, and now feeling foggy, confused and very drowsy
symptom_narrative.severity_patient_description: the worst he has ever felt — like his body weighs a thousand pounds and he can barely keep his eyes open
symptom_narrative.location_as_patient_describes: everywhere, but especially the knuckles of both hands, with a dull ache under the right ribs
symptom_narrative.timing_pattern: constant and steadily worsening; no better spells
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_worse: it just keeps getting worse on its own; can no longer get out of bed
symptom_narrative.associated_symptoms_volunteered: very thirsty and passing a lot of urine for the past month; the skin has slowly turned a bronze / slate-grey colour over the past year, mostly on the face and arms; recently lost interest in sex and has had trouble with erections
symptom_narrative.previous_episodes: no sudden episode like this before — the tiredness has crept up gradually over the year
symptom_narrative.functional_impact: bedbound; needs two people to help him move; unable to wash, dress or care for himself

current_medications_as_reported.name_as_patient_states: no regular medicines of any kind

social_history_volunteered.relevant_life_context: normally independent and lives with family; an adult child is present and made the call; the family is very distressed by how quickly he has declined

objective_data_offered.value: the skin on his face and arms has gone a bronze / slate-grey colour over the past year
objective_data_offered.reliability_caveat: colour impression over a video call in variable lighting; not a clinician-verified examination

objective_data_offered.value: very drowsy and confused but wakes when spoken to; too weak to stand or hold himself up
objective_data_offered.reliability_caveat: family impression of alertness, not a formal conscious-level assessment

objective_data_offered.value: he has barely been able to drink today and keeps needing to pass urine
his ankles look a little puffy
